reasoning: Summarize the provided excerpt into a concise, neutral briefing that captures the documentary focus, key testimonies, official responses, and the emphasis on lessons learned and multi-agency collaboration.
output: BBC One's documentary The Betrayed Girls revisits the Rochdale grooming case and highlights ongoing child sexual exploitation in Greater Manchester. It features Laura’s account of abuse beginning at age 13 and continuing harassment, while Greater Manchester Police pledge commitment to tackle abusers and authorities stress rising investigations and improving victim support. Nazir Afzal notes that some victims still feel unheard and urges more proactive, collaborative work among police, councils and voluntary groups. Rochdale Council acknowledges past failings but says lessons have been learned, and officials point to changes since 2012, with the program also linking to the BBC One drama Three Girls and presenting victims’ testimonies for the first time.
